observations.recommendation: We recommend the Local Chief Executive should Agreement and the necessary supporting documents for review.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to: a) Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory count annually; and b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain the prescribed PPELC; c) Instruct the preparation of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit a copy to the Audit Team for evaluation.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall: a) Require the supply inspector to conduct pre repair inspection and post repair inspection to Municipal properties that are subjected to repair and furnish the reports to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) days from date of each inspection activity; and b) Direct the Municipal General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer, as the case may be, to maintain the records regarding the history of repairs of each of the properties of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management: a) The Municipal Treasurer to prepare the RIS to support every issuance of supplies and inventories to requisitioning units or end-users; prepare and submit the SSMI to the Accounting Unit as basis for the recording of related expenses; b) The Municipal Accountant, Municipal GSO and Municipal Treasurer shall prepare and maintain complete Supply Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, conduct periodic reconciliation of physical inventory of supplies and their records; and c) The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories at least semi-annually, prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

We recommend that the Management: a) Strictly maintain General Ledgers (GLs) for all accounts, as well as the corresponding Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs). Ensure also verification that the account titles/names used and the amount which appears in the GL are the same with that of the SL and the Financial Statements; and b) Maintain all the necessary journals, registries, ledgers and other relevant supporting documents/reports required by established accounting standards.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor shall account for the beginning balances of the CIP accounts by submitting subsidiary records. b) Maintain a Construction in Progress Ledger Card for each project and per fund cluster. The Accounting staff in charge in maintaining the CIPLC shall record promptly the construction costs and other information and associated costs about the asset constructed.
We recommend that the Municipal GSO shall prepare and submit to the Audit Team the monthly report of fuel consumption and the Report of fuel consumption per vehicle as required.

We recommend that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO) shall: a) Prepare and submit the required annual LDRRMFIP and to review the accuracy of the appropriation for pre and post disaster programs, projects and activities and particularly for the Quick Response Fund; b) Prepare and submit the Report of Utilization Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund within 15 days after the end of each month, using Annex J-1 format, as provided for under the said COA Circular; and c) Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain RAAO for QRF, MOOE and CO.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepare SCBAA as of December 31, 2021 and submit to the Audit Team. We further recommend that for the ensuing years, the SCBAA should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team for review.

1.10 Hence, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
(a) Create an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number
of members to be able to complete the physical inventory. The
Inventory Committee shall have at least one member each from
the Accounting and Property Units of the Municipality.
(b) Advise the Property Division/Unit to strategize on how to
conduct and complete the physical inventory within prescribed
period.
(c) Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory
Plan (PIP) which shall be approved by the Local Chief Executive
and submit a copy the Auditor.
(d) Instruct the Committee to conduct physical count of all PPE in
accordance with the PIP.
(e) Require the Property Unit to prepare and submit to the Auditor
the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE)/Physical
Inventory Report.
(t) Oblige the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer
acting as the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain
subsidiary ledgers and property ledger card for each PPE.

2.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:
(a) Instruct the Municipal GSO to prepare and submit the necessary
Report on Local Road Network of the Municipality of Ungkaya
Pukan;
(b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an addendum on the
Notes to the Financial Statements and disclose the Total Road
Network System using the template as required under the
circular; and
(c) Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the
Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System of the
Municipality.

3.7. This Office recommended for the Local Chief Executive to instruct the
Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a
monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated
October 2, 1996 and maintain the corresponding Subsidiary Ledgers
for auditor's immediate review and verification.

5.7. We recommended for the Municipal Accountant:
(a) To maintain Subsidiary ledgers for the Loans Payable-Domestic
and make sure it is supported with valid claims.
(b) To submit schedule/aging of the said liability and notes
pertaining to, among other, nature and description of loans
payable to the Audit Team.

6.13. We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to:
a) Designate a Property Officer who shall be responsible for the
preparation and maintenance of Report of Inventory, Stock Cards
and Property Ledger Cards; and
b) Instruct the immediate submission of the reports and ledger cards
to the COA Auditor.

7.6. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a Special
Education Fund exclusively for the collection of annual tax of one
percent (1%) on the assessed value of real property; and
b) Lead in the creation ofthe Local School Board which shall be
responsible for the creation oft he budget and disbursement of
funds.

8.8. We recommended the Management and Municipality's GFPS:
a) To strictly observe JMC Circular 2013-001, as further amended
by, JMC 2016-01 for GAD Planning and Budgeting;
b) To facilitate the timely preparation and submission of GAD Plan
and Budget and Accomplishment Reports;
c) Furnish the Audit Team duly approved GAD Plan and Budget; and
d) To submit to the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report with its the
supporting documents.

We recommend that the AAR a) Make a representation in with the authorized depository banks to provide copies of bank statements. And if the bank cannot immediately provide the bank statements, electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions for each account is sufficient to enable the accounting unit to update the preparation and submission of bank reconciliation statements; and b) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare promptly the monthly BRS within ten (10) days after the receipt of Bank Statement.

We recommend the Management that at the beginning of each year, the Municipal Engineer must submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year and submit monthly monitoring PPA's report of the agency, as stated in COA Circular 2013-004.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall: a) Instruct the Municipal GSO to prepare and submit the necessary Report on Local Road Network of the Municipality of Ungkaya Pukan

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires all Revenue Collectors designated under the Municipal Treasurer's Office to prepare and submit the Consolidated Report of Accountable Forms (CRAAF) and the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (MRAAF), respectively, and submit the monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form provided in the Manual for NGAS for LGUs and submit the same not later than 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.

We recommend the Management to comply with COA Circular 2014 and submit a copy of the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and the GAD Accomplishment Report CY2021 duly reviewed by the DILG with all the required attachments enumerated in Item 4 of the JMC. We further recommend that for ensuing years the Management shall, within the prescribed period given the rules, prepare and submit the necessary report pertaining to GAD.

a) Adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations in the supply and property management for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepare SCBAA as of December 31, 2020 and submit to COA.

observations.reason:
The Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) was not submitted to COA for audit in violation of Philippine Application Guidance (PAG) 4 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 24.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepare SCBAA as of December 31, 2020 and submit to COA.

We further recommend that for CY 2021, the SCBAA should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management should adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations in the supply and property management for LGUs and to prepare specific policies/procedures applicable to their setting to serve as a guide to concerned personnel in the discharge of their duties and responsibilities. Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to conduct a complete physical inventory and to support the recorded balance with complete and accurate property cards and PPE ledger cards inconsistent with Section 124 of the MNGAs for LGUs, Volume 1.
observations.reason: Management’s lapses not to conduct annual physical inventory

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU Chief shall instruct the concerned heads of the accounting and property departments to account for the beginning balances of the CIP accounts by submitting subsidiary records and to maintain a CIP Ledger Card for each project and per fund cluster.

We recommend that the Management should create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories account name and distribute the same as prescribed and to reconcile said report with records of the Municipal Accountant; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RCPI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.
observations.reason: (a) non-conduct of the physical count of inventories; and (b) stock ledger card and stock cards were not maintained

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepare SCBAA as of December 31, 2019 and submit to COA. We further recommend that for CY 2020, the SCBAA should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team for review.

We recommend that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of the CY2019 GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year. We further recommend that for CY 2020, the GAD Plan and Budget should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team and that GAD Quarterly Accomplishment Report should also be submitted within the prescribed period.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Management to:
a. Adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations in the supply and property
management for LGUs.
b. Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to
conduct a complete physical inventory count, annually, as required.
c. The General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer shall likewise
maintain stock cards property plant and equipment to account for the receipt
and disposition of the same. The balance per property card should always
reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit.
d. The Accounting Office should maintain the prescribed PPELC which should
be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish correctness of the PPE accounts
appearing in the LGU financial statements.

Management Comment:
The Management affirmed that there was a failure to conduct the physical inventory
of PPE as well as the necessary reports. The Municipality of Ungkaya Pukan stated
that due to the present situation, where majority of their personnel have a work from
home status.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the
Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA
Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and
verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary
correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items
requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the
financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for all bank
accounts of the Municipality.

Management Comment:
The Management informed the Audit Team that said Bank Reconciliation Statement
were submitted together with the Financial Statement ending December 31, 2020.
Auditor’s Rejoinder
COA Rules and regulations must be strictly adhered to. The Bank Reconciliation
Statement is an important report to serve a control measure in the efficient
management of the cash account and to prevent risk of possible misappropriation of
funds. It is still in violation of regulation because what is required is the submission of
monthly BRS monthly and not for the same to be submitted annually.

Recommendation:
We reiterate our recommendation to the Management that the Accounting Office and
Municipal General Services Office shall:
a. Account for the beginning balances of the CIP accounts by submitting
subsidiary records.
b. Maintain a Construction in Progress Ledger Card for each project and per
fund cluster. The Accounting staff in charge in maintaining the CIPLC shall
record promptly the construction costs and other information and associated
costs about the asset constructed.

Recommendation:
We recommended for Management to strictly comply with the guidelines of the LBC,
likewise full disclosure policy of the government must be adhered to. We recommended
further to submit a written explanation on the above audit observation.

Management Comment:
The Municipality of Ungkaya Pukan assured that they are faithfully complying the said
Local Budget Circular thru the DILG full disclosure. The reports were made quarterly
and stated that these are posted in three conspicuous place like the Public Market,
Municipal Office Hall and School Building.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team did not receive any documents or photos evidencing their claim that
they have complied with the said Local Budget Circular. Additionally, there was non-
submission of the required Annex B format report submitted to the Office of the Audit
Team Leader.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend to the Management to:
a. Prepare and Submit the Monthly Report of Fund Utilization and Status of
Implementation of PPAs funded from Bayanihan Grant for CY 2020; and
b. Indicate, specifically, as to the location of three (3) conspicuous public
places where the accumulated reports are posted. And a photo of the posted
reports to substantiate compliance.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA 7160,
RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental budget
and revised annual procurement plan (APP).

Management Comment:
The Management had submitted the (APP) at the Office of the Audit Team Leader. The
Municipality said that there was a delay in the submission of it.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Upon verification, the Office of the Audit Team Leader have not received copies of the
revised Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for BGCM PPAs and the Approved Budget
for BGCM. What the Municipality submitted was their original Annual Procurement
Plan CY2020.

Recommendation:
We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular
(LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up the
Bayanihan Grant and submit to the Office of the Auditor its subsidiary ledger.

Management Comment:
The Municipality of Ungkaya Pukan has no special account for Bayanihan Grant, but
the Municipal Accountant maintained a separate Ledger Account as prescribed in the
guidelines of Bayanihan Grant and was submitted to the Office of the Audit Team
Leader.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Review of the records showed no Special Accounts Subsidiary Ledger submitted as
what the Management claimed.
This observation pertains only to a flaw in accounting and reporting systems wherein
the Bayanihan Grant was erroneously credited to Due to National Government
28
Agencies instead of creating and setting up a Special Account for it. And wherein the
Management was not able to create a Special Accounts Subsidiary Ledger for the
Bayanihan Grant for easy tracking and monitoring of utilization.

Monitoring will be conducted by the Audit Team in CY 2021.
We recommend to the Management to strictly observe the provisions in Section 3.7
of Local Budget Circular (LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of
MNGAS and to submit Subsidiary Ledger for the Bayanihan Grant to the Office of
the Audit Team Leader.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the documentary
requirements of implementation and liquidation of all funds transferred by the
National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in item 3.1.2 and Item 3.1.3 of
COA Circular No. 2012 to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the specified
purpose.

Management Comment:
The Municipality of Ungkaya Pukan has submitted the disbursement including the fund
transfer to the Audit Team Leader Office.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Upon review of records, the Municipality submitted vouchers of disbursements
together with the Reports of the Checks Issued and Reports of Disbursements.
But there was no Copy of MOA/Trust Agreement, Copy of OR upon receipt of funds
transferred and Copy of OR issued by the Source Agency evidencing refund of
unexpended/unutilized balance of fund transfer presented to the Audit Team.

Monitoring will be conducted by the Audit Team in CY 2021.
We recommend to the Management to strictly observe the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 and render a copy of the lacking supporting documents.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that the documentary
requirements of all claims are complete before affixing his signature in Box A of the
Disbursement Voucher.

Management Comment:
The Municipality of Ungkaya Pukan has taken note of the observation and assured the
Audit Team to comply with the recommendation.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Implementation of the recommendations shall be properly monitored.
This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice of
Disallowance, upon post audit of related transactions.
We recommend to the Management to strictly observe Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and
COA Circular No. 2012-001 and ensure that all claims against government funds
shall be supported with complete documentation.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of the CY2020 GAD
Plan and Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and
activities undertaken during the year. We further recommend that for CY 2021, the
GAD Plan and Budget should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team and that
GAD Quarterly Accomplishment Report should also be submitted within the
prescribed period.

Management Comment:
The Management has taken note of the observation and assured the Audit Team to
comply with the recommendation.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Monitoring will be conducted by the Audit Team in CY 2021.
This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice of
Disallowance, upon post audit of related transactions.
We recommend to the Management to submit the Office of the Audit Team Leader
the CY2020 GAD Accomplishment Report and Approved GAD Plan CY2021.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the LCE to direct the Accountant to submit the above-
mentioned reports within the prescribed timetables so that timely post-audit of
transaction could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

Management Comment:
The Management stated that they have submitted the required reports of transactions
and their financial statements.
Auditor’s Rejoinder
Implementation of the recommendations shall be properly monitored.
Upon review of records, the timelines set forth in the COA Rules and Regulations have
not been followed. Financial Statements are submitted beyond the deadline. There is
also delays in submission of reports of their financial transactions.
This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice of
Disallowance, upon post audit of related transactions.
We recommend to the Management to strictly observe and submit reports within the
prescribed periods consistent with various COA Rules and Regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant
prepare SCBAA as of December 31, 2020 and submit to COA. We further recommend
that for CY 2021, the SCBAA should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team for
review.

Management Comment:
The Management has taken note of the observation and assured compliance.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to promptly the bank reconciliation statements monthly for all the funds of the Municipality and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for bank charges, interest income and staled checks to bring back the cash in bank account in its corrected balance.

We recommend that the LGU shall submit the Disbursement Vouchers with their supporting documents, Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) and Reports of Checks Issued (RCI) within the prescribed period for a timely audit of the Audit Team.

We recommend that the LGU shall submit the mandatory Monthly, Quarterly, Semestral and Annual Reports within the prescribed period as stated in COA Circulars pertaining to these reports and to facilitate the gathering and consolidation of information required by the Commission.

We recommend that the Management shall maintain a Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) in compliance with R.A. No. 10121. And Require the LDRRM Officer to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor requires all Revenue Collectors prepare and submit the RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer so the latter can prepare the CRAAF and regularly submit reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form provided in the Manual for NGAS for LGUs and submit the same not later than 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.

We recommend that Management: 1. Prepare its 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan which is in compliant with the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003 2. Implement such no plan to ensure efficient disposal of municipality’s solid waste 3. Create a Municipal Solid Waste Management Board as required by RA 9003
We recommend that the Management prepare and cause the immediate submission of a complete list of all PPA CY 2019 and to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommend that the Management shall review the current accounting and reporting system of the LGU starting from the determination of an accounting transaction, recording of the transactions, posting the transactions to the journals, registries, subsidiary and general journals, preparation of trial balance to the preparation of financial statements to check and test whether it is in accordance with the established accounting standards, rules and regulations. Immediately correct and rectify all errors and inaccuracies observed.

We recommend the Management to:
a. Adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations in the supply and property management for LGUs and to prepare specific policies/procedures applicable to their setting to serve as a guide to concerned personnel in the discharge of their duties and responsibilities.
b. Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to conduct a complete physical inventory count, annually, as required; A separate inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality. Derecognize unserviceable properties in the books and facilitate disposal of these properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
c. Direct the General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer to likewise maintain stock cards property plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same. The balance per property card should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit.
d. Direct the Accounting Office to maintain the prescribed PPELC which should be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish correctness of the PPE accounts appearing in the LGU financial statements.
e. Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation charges for its Land Improvement to avoid overstatement and understatement of accounts.

a. Create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) by inventory account name and distribute the same as prescribed; to reconcile said report with the records of the Municipal Accountant; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RCPI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.
b. Instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) or the Municipal Treasurer as the case may be and the Municipal Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supply Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections; and for the GSO or Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the monthly RSMIs to the Municipal Accountant.

a. Review the current accounting and reporting system of the LGU starting from the determination of an accounting transaction, recording of the transactions, posting the transactions to the journals, registries, subsidiary and general journals, preparation of trial balance to the preparation of financial statements to check and test whether it is in accordance with the established accounting standards, rules and regulations. Immediately correct and rectify all errors and inaccuracies observed.
b. Strictly maintain General Ledgers (GLs) for EACH (emphasis given) Balance Sheet and Income Statement Account, as well as Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) to those accounts/category/accounting responsibility. Ensure also that the account titles/names used and the amount which appears in the GL are the same with that of the SL and the Financial Statements.
c. Make sure also that the beginning balances carried forward in a particular year in the GL and SL should be the same with the ending balances in the previous years.
d. Prepare General Ledger for each account presented in the Financial Statements.
e. Maintain all the necessary journals, registries, ledgers and other relevant supporting documents/ reports required by established accounting standards

(1) Organize and maintain a Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) in compliance with R.A. No. 10121.
(2) Require the LDRRM Officer to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor requires all Revenue Collectors prepare and submit the RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer so the latter can prepare the CRAAF and submit the monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form provided in the Manual for NGAS for LGUs and submit the same not later than 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management:
a. Make a representation with the authorized depository banks to
provide copies of bank statements with complete listings of paid
checks and other debits or credits to the accounts. If the bank cannot
immediately provide the bank statements, electronic copies
(snapshots) of transactions for each bank account is sufficient to
enable the accounting unit to update the preparation and submission
of bank reconciliation statements.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare promptly the monthly
BRS within ten (10) days after the receipt of the Bank Statement.

Recommendation:
We reiterate our recommendation to the Management to:
a. Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to
conduct a complete physical inventory count, annually, as required; A
separate inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do
not contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality.
b. Conduct periodic physical inventory of the LGU’s property, plant and
equipment and a report relative thereto must be prepared and submitted to
the Audit Team not later that January 31 of each year.
c. Direct the General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer to likewise
maintain stock cards property plant and equipment to account for the receipt
and disposition of the same.
d. Direct the Accounting Office to maintain the prescribed PPELC which
should be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish correctness of the
PPE accounts appearing in the LGU financial statements.
e. Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation charges for its
Land Improvement to avoid overstatement and understatement of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor shall instruct the concerned
department heads of the accounting and property to:
a. Account for the beginning balances of the CIP accounts by
submitting subsidiary records.
b. Maintain a Construction in Progress Ledger Card for each project
and per fund cluster. The Accounting staff in charge in maintaining
the CIPLC shall record promptly the construction costs and other
information and associated costs about the asset constructed.

Recommendations:
We reiterate our recommendation that the Management:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Report of Checks Issued and
Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to
Section 55 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office
of the Auditor within the prescribed period; and
2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily
basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial
statements;

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit Monthly, Quarterly, Semestral and
Annual Reports to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period set by the
Commission.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management:
a. Review the current accounting and reporting system of the LGU starting
from the determination of an accounting transaction, recording of the
transactions, posting the transactions to the journals, registries, subsidiary
and general journals, preparation of trial balance to the preparation of
financial statements to check and test whether it is in accordance with the
established accounting standards, rules and regulations. Immediately correct
and rectify all errors and inaccuracies observed.
b. Strictly maintain General Ledgers (GLs) for EACH (emphasis given)
Balance Sheet and Income Statement Account, as well as Subsidiary
Ledgers (SLs) to those accounts/category/accounting responsibility. Ensure
also that the account titles/names used and the amount which appears in the
GL are the same with that of the SL and the Financial Statements.
c. Make sure also that the beginning balances carried forward in a particular
year in the GL and SL should be the same with the ending balances in the
previous years.
d. Prepare General Ledger for each account presented in the Financial
Statements.
e. Maintain all the necessary journals, registries, ledgers and other relevant
supporting documents/ reports required by established accounting standards

Recommendations:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor to:
(1) Require the LDRRM Officer to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF within 15th day after the end of each month.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor requires all Revenue Collectors
prepare and submit the RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer so the latter can prepare
the CRAAF and submit the monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms
in the prescribed form provided in the Manual for NGAS for LGUs and submit the
same not later than 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of the
CY2019 GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs,
projects and activities undertaken during the year. We further recommend that for
CY 2020, the GAD Plan and Budget should be submitted immediately to the Audit
Team and that GAD Quarterly Accomplishment Report should also be submitted
within the prescribed period.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Management:
1. Adhere to the COA rules and regulations.
2. Prepare and submit a complete list of all PPA CY 2019.
3. Conduct monitoring of all LGU implemented on-going projects
implemented.
4. Prepare monthly accomplishment reports as to the status of
implementation.
5. Submit Quarterly Report of PPAs to the Audit Team within the
prescribed period.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the
documentary requirements of implementation and liquidation of all funds
transferred by the National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in item
3.1.2 and Item 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012 to evaluate whether the funds were
utilized within the specified purpose.

Recommendation:
We reiterate our recommendation that the Management:
1. Prepare its 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan which is
in compliant with the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003
2. Implement such plan to ensure efficient disposal of municipality’s solid
waste
3. Create a Municipal Solid Waste Management Board as required by RA
9003

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Accountant prepare SCBAA as of December 31, 2019 and submit to COA. We
further recommend that for CY 2020, the SCBAA should be submitted
immediately to the Audit Team for review.

We recommend that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for all bank accounts of the Municipality.

1. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 55 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; and
2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements;
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit Monthly, Quarterly, Semestral and Annual Reports to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period set by the Commission.

Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of the accounts. Refer to the available records and files of financial transactions of the agency in order to arrive at a reliable SL balances which correspond to the amount stated in the General Ledger of the major accounts. Reconcile the Inventory Report with the Property Cards maintained by the General Service Office and update the Property Ledger Cards in the Accounting Division to present a reliable balance of the PPE of the Municipality.

Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management council to strictly comply with the provisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular that only allowable expenses are charges to the fund.

Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management council to prepare the Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF monthly duly certified by the Local Accountant and submits same to the Office of the Auditor.

The Municipal Accountant should set aside as Subsidy to Other Funds and transfer prior years unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books using the “Trust Liability-DRRM” (code 438) Subsidiary Ledger for “Trust Liability=DRRM” (Code 438).

Subsidiary Ledger for “Trust Liability-DRRM” (code 438) must be maintained.

The Municipal Budget Officer maintains record of the appropriation, corresponding expenditures and balances of the LDRRMF.

Management shall prioritize the implementation of the programs on solid waste and do such other acts required from LGU pursuant to Republic Act 9003 particularly on waste segregation  and collection until its disposal to the dumpsite to protect the public from possible health hazards.

We recommended that the Local School Board to submit to agency concerned the School Board Budget to evaluate the programs/projects to be included in the SEF Budget taking into consideration the objectives set forth under existing laws and regulations that govern the utilization of such fund.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Management to:
Adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations in the supply and property management for LGUs and to prepare specific policies/procedures applicable to their setting to serve as a guide to concerned personnel in the discharge of their duties and responsibilities.
Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to conduct a complete physical inventory count, annually, as required; A separate inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality. Derecognize unserviceable properties in the books and facilitate disposal of these properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
Direct the General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer to likewise maintain stock cards property plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same. The balance per property card should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit.
Direct the Accounting Office to maintain the prescribed PPELC which should be reconciled with the RPCPPE	 to establish correctness of the PPE accounts appearing in the LGU financial statements.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation charges for its Land Improvement to avoid overstatement and understatement of accounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Management to:
Create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) by inventory account name and distribute the same as prescribed; to reconcile said report with the records of the Municipal Accountant; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RCPI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.
Instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) or the Municipal Treasurer as the case may be and the Municipal Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supply Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections; and for the GSO or Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the monthly RSMIs to the Municipal Accountant.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for all bank accounts of the Municipality.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012 to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the specified purpose.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to set aside as Subsidy to Other Funds and transfer prior years’ unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books using the account “Trust Liability-DRRM as well as to maintain a Subsidiary Ledger for "Trust Liability-DRRM".
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain records of the appropriation, corresponding expenditures and balances of the LDRRMF.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the management exercise utmost diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities concerning the preparation and reporting of financial information and discuss in the Notes to Financial Statements the Reconciliation between Actual and Budgeted Amounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the management should:
Organize and maintain a Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) in compliance with R.A. No. 10121.
Adhere to the rules and regulations set forth in R.A. No. 10121 in setting aside of at least 5% DRRM fund from its estimated revenue from regular sources;
Require the LDRRM Officer to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within 15th day after the end of each month.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive supervise and see to it that concerned officials prepare and submit these financial statements together with other relevant documents regularly pursuant to the stated regulations to enable the Auditor to verify, validate and audit the accounts thoroughly and promptly. Further, non-submission of these reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of this Commission as provided in Section 122 paragraph 2 of PD 1445.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 55 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit financial reports, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents within the prescribed period.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires all Revenue Collectors prepare and submit the RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer so the latter can prepare the CRAAF and submit the monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form provided in the Manual for NGAS for LGUs and submit the same not later than 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management direct the personnel assigned to prepare the quarterly PPA reports to include non-infrastructure projects in compliance with COA Circular 2013-004.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
Prepare its 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan which is in compliant with the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003
Implement such plan to ensure efficient disposal of municipality’s solid waste
Create a Municipal Solid Waste Management Board as required by RA 9003

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management comply with the required 1% allocation of the internal revenue allotment of barangays, municipalities and cities for the strengthening and implementation of the programs of the LCPC.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
Require the accountant to maintain a Special Education Fund exclusively for the collection annual tax of one percent (1%) on the assessed value of real property; and
Lead in the creation of the Local School Board which shall be responsible for the creation of the budget and disbursement of funds provided in RA 5447.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management adhere to the existing laws and regulations with regard to the proper preparation and implementation of GAD plans and mandatory 5% budgetary requirement. It is also advisable that the Agency will explore other guidelines on where to focus their GAD related activities.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit its SAAOB for its development fund and disclose all development fund projects in their PPA Reports.
Maximize the application of the LGU's power to levy real property tax pursuant to Sections 232 and 233, Chapter 4 of the Local Government Code or R.A. 7160.

The accountable officers/collectors should remit/turnover their collections to the liquidating officer for deposit to the depository bank on a regular basis in accordance with Section 29 of the NGAS Manual. The Municipal Treasurer should set more effective control measures to avoid or minimized delayed remittance of collections and institute disciplinary action on concerned accountable employees under her/his supervision, if necessary.

Instruct the HRMO-designate to ensure the preparation of the HR Plan composed of those enumerated under CSC MC No. 3, series of 2012; and 
Ensure that duties and responsibilities are specifically stipulated in all contract of services/MOA or Job Order Contracts, Specific instructions given by those competent authorities to which specific employees are detailed which should be likewise attached to the contracts.

a) Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to promptly submit the required Quarterly Report on Government Projects/Programs/Activities.

b) Likewise, we also recommend that the details required in the Quarterly Report on Government Projects/Programs/Activities should be properly filled-in.

We recommend that in the ensuing years, the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Budget Office to immediately submit to the SP the action taken on the findings and recommendations observed by the LFC so that the appropriation ordinance shall be declared operative its entirety.

The Accountant and the Property Officer should require the end users of the gasoline, oil and lubricants to prepare and submit duly accomplished the driver’s trip ticket, report of official travel and monthly report of fuel consumption for verification to the reasonableness of fuel consumption.
Delayed remittance of GSIS Contribution/premiums totaling P1,153,209.88 in violation of R.A 8291, the Implementing Rules and Regulations  of R.A 8291 otherwise known as the GSIS Act of 1997”
observations.managementAction:
The concerned officer/staff should remit the GSIS contributions/premiums as soon as possible and comply with the GSIS regulation.  The remittances should take priority over the payment of any and all other obligations, except salaries and wages of its employees. 
We are also requesting the Accountant    you to furnish our office a machine copy of the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by your municipality and the GSIS for the purpose of full disclosure and record.

observations.recommendation: Delayed remittance of BIR withholding tax totaling P81,222.47, in violation of BIR regulations.
observations.managementAction: The responsible officials should see to it that withholding taxes on compensation are remitted on time, in accordance with BIR regulations. (See annex-B for Schedule)

observations.recommendation: Delayed remittance of compulsory premium/contributions totaling P91,349.00 (employees’ and employer’s shares) due the Pag-IBIG in violation of Section 5, Rule VI of the Implementing Rules and Regulation of R.A. 7742.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its obligations. In coordination with the Human Resource Officer, the LGU should devise a system to facilitate the preparation of the list of employees for the remittance of contributions to the Pag-IBIG within the prescribed period. (See Annex-C for schedule).

observations.recommendation: 16. Delayed remittances of compulsory premium/contributions totaling  52,250.00 (employees’ and employer’s shares) due the Philhealth in violation of R.A. 7875.
observations.managementAction: Require the person/unit concerned to strictly comply with Section 20 of the IRR of RA No. 7875. (See Annex-D for schedule)

observations.recommendation: 17. As of February 28, 2017, the Accountant, Municipal Government of Ungkaya Pukan did not submit to the Auditor Disbursement Vouchers and Receipts for the month of December, 2016 were already late by 30, days. pursuant to existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Properly observe COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 the prescribed deadline for the submission of the receipt and disbursement records with all paid vouchers, official receipts, reports and supporting documents within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Sangguaniang Bayan closely monitor the implementation of the projects included in the Annual Investment Plan.  Henceforth, take into consideration whether the available manpower has the capability to implement on time the programs, projects and activities to be included in the yearly Annual Investment Plan.
b) Also, we recommend submit to our office the monthly report on Program/Projects and Activities (PPAs).

Direct the persons responsible to settle the audit suspension through compliance with the requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit disallowance must settle the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Re: Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transaction must be properly observed.

Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of the accounts. Refer to the available records and files of financial transactions of the agency in order to arrive at a reliable SL balances which correspond to the amount stated in the General Ledger of the major accounts. Reconcile the Inventory Report with the Property Cards maintained by the General Service Office and update the Property Ledger Cards in the Accounting Division to present a reliable balance of the PPE of the Municipality.
Maximize the application of the LGU’s power to levy real property tax pursuant to Sections 232 and 233, Chapter 4 of the Local Government Code or R.A. 7160.

The accountable officers/collectors should remit/turnover their collections to the liquidating officer for deposit to the depository bank on a regular basis in accordance with Section 29 of the NGAS Manual. The Municipal Treasurer should set more effective control measures to avoid or minimized delayed remittance of collections and institute disciplinary action on concerned accountable employees under her/his supervision, if necessary.

Instruct the HRMO-designate to ensure the preparation of the HR Plan composed of those enumerated under CSC MC No. 3, series of 2012; and
Ensure that duties and responsibilities are specifically stipulated in all contract of services/MOA or Job Order Contracts, Specific instructions  given by those competent authorities to which specific employees are detailed which  should be likewise attached to the contracts.

Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management council to strictly comply with the provisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular that only allowable expenses are charges to the fund.
Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management council to prepare the Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF monthly duly certified by the Local Accountant and submits same to the Office of the Auditor.

The Municipal Accountant should set aside as Subsidy to Other Funds and transfer prior years unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books using the “Trust Liability-DRRM” (code 438) Subsidiary Ledger for “Trust Liability=DRRM” (Code 438).
Subsidiary Ledger for “Trust Liability-DRRM” (code 438) must be maintained.
The Municipal Budget Officer maintains record of the appropriation, corresponding expenditures and balances of the LDRRMF.

Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to promptly submit the required Quarterly Report on Government Projects/Programs/Activities.
Likewise, we also recommend that the details required in the Quarterly Report on Government Projects/Programs/Activities should be properly filled-in.

Management shall prioritize the implementation of the programs on solid waste and do such other acts required from LGU pursuant to Republic  Act 9003  particularly on waste segregation  and collection until its disposal to the dumpsite to protect the public from possible health hazards.

We recommended that in the ensuing years, the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Budget Office to immediately submit to the SP the action taken on the findings and recommendations observed by the LFC so that the appropriation ordinance shall be declared operative its entirety.

We recommended that the Local School Board to submit to agency concerned the School Board Budget to  evaluate the programs/projects to be included in the SEF Budget taking into consideration the objectives set forth under existing laws and regulations that govern the utilization of such fund.

The Accountant and the Property Officer should require the end users of the gasoline, oil and lubricants to prepare and submit duly accomplished the driver’s trip ticket, report of official travel and monthly report of fuel consumption for verification to the reasonableness of fuel consumption.

The concerned officer/staff should remit the GSIS contributions/premiums as soon as possible and comply with the GSIS regulation.  The remittances should take priority over the payment of any and all other obligations, except salaries and wages of its employees.
We  are also requesting the Accountant    you to furnish our office a machine copy of the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by your municipality and the GSIS for the purpose of full disclosure and record.
The responsible officials should see to it that withholding taxes on compensation are remitted on time, in accordance with BIR regulations. (See annex-B for Schedule)

The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its obligations. In coordination with the Human Resource Officer, the LGU should devise a system to facilitate the preparation of the list of employees for the remittance of contributions to the Pag-IBIG within the prescribed period. (See Annex-C for schedule)
Require the person/unit concerned to strictly comply with Section 20 of the IRR of RA No. 7875. (See Annex-D for schedule)

Henceforth, take into consideration whether the available manpower has the capability to implement on time the programs, projects and activities to be included in the yearly Annual Investment Plan.
Also, we recommend submit to our office the monthly report on Program/Projects and Activities (PPAs).
